title: Refactor tui/app.py to reduce complexity
description: src/agent_takt/tui/app.py is 1,132 lines and acts as a god class containing the Textual App class, all keybindings, TuiSchedulerReporter, and accumulated functionality. The TUI module has been partially decomposed (state.py, render.py, actions.py, tree.py) but app.py remains the bottleneck. Extract TuiSchedulerReporter into its own module (tui/reporter.py) since it is a self-contained class with no dependency on the App internals. Extract keybinding registration and the BINDINGS table into a dedicated section or helper. Goal: reduce app.py below 700 lines without changing any observable behaviour. All existing TUI tests must continue to pass. Preserve all public re-exports in tui/__init__.py.

handoff_summary.completed: 1. Created tui/reporter.py with TuiSchedulerReporter class (65 lines) — self-contained, no App internals dependency. 2. Added 6 helper functions to tui/render.py: _panel_badge, _format_filter_label, _beads_panel_title, _detail_summary_lines, _detail_section_body, _detail_section_title (+111 lines). 3. Added OrchestratorTuiActionsMixin class to tui/actions.py containing all 27 action_* methods from OrchestratorTuiApp (+290 lines). Mixin uses optional textual imports guarded by try/except (safe: methods only run from a live Textual app). BeadTree type annotations replaced with Tree base class; HelpOverlay instantiation delegated to _make_help_overlay_screen() factory on the main class. 4. Stripped app.py of all extracted code; updated imports to point at new homes; changed OrchestratorTuiApp to inherit from OrchestratorTuiActionsMixin; added 2-line _make_help_overlay_screen() factory method. 5. Updated tui/__init__.py to re-export TuiSchedulerReporter from .reporter and _detail_section_body/_detail_section_title from .render — all previously-stable public symbols remain accessible from agent_takt.tui.
handoff_summary.remaining: None. Tester bead should run the existing TUI test suite (test_tui_app.py, test_tui_render.py, test_tui_actions.py, test_tui_state.py, test_tui_tree.py) to confirm no regressions.
handoff_summary.risks: The mixin uses # type: ignore[attr-defined] on every self.* call because mypy cannot verify the OrchestratorTuiApp attributes at mixin definition time. This is intentional and consistent with the project's typing approach. No runtime risk: methods only execute from a running App instance.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run tui test suite to verify no regressions: uv run pytest tests/test_tui_app.py tests/test_tui_render.py tests/test_tui_actions.py tests/test_tui_state.py tests/test_tui_tree.py -v
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: tui/__init__.py and tui/app.py are the most likely points of conflict for downstream beads importing from the tui package. The public API surface is unchanged — all symbols remain re-exported from the same package namespace.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: 1. Mixin pattern (OrchestratorTuiActionsMixin) for action methods: chosen because action methods are a coherent unit (all named action_*) that reference self.runtime_state and self._* helpers via duck typing. Alternative was standalone functions taking the app as a parameter, rejected as more verbose. 2. BeadTree replaced with Tree in mixin query_one calls: BeadTree is an inner class scoped to build_tui_app; Tree is the public Textual base class and has all required methods (action_cursor_down, action_toggle_node etc.). No behavioural change. 3. _make_help_overlay_screen() factory: added to OrchestratorTuiApp (2 lines) to let the mixin trigger HelpOverlay creation without importing the locally-scoped inner class. 4. Optional textual imports in actions.py: textual is an optional dependency; the try/except guard ensures actions.py can be imported without textual installed. The mixin methods are only ever called from a live App so NoMatches etc. will always be defined at call time. 5. _panel_badge, _detail_section_body etc. placed in render.py (not a new file): they are rendering helpers that fit the module's existing responsibilities and require no new imports.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) All 27 action methods in the mixin still function correctly via the inherited OrchestratorTuiApp path — existing test_tui_app.py covers most action handlers. (2) TuiSchedulerReporter can still be imported from agent_takt.tui and behaves identically (TuiSchedulerReporterDeferredTests class in test_tui_app.py). (3) _detail_section_body and _detail_section_title remain accessible from agent_takt.tui with the same results. (4) _live_status_bar_text is still importable from agent_takt.tui.app (direct import in tests). (5) build_tui_app returns a functioning OrchestratorTuiApp that inherits both the mixin actions and App base class.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: BINDINGS table remains inside OrchestratorTuiApp (nested in build_tui_app) because it uses Binding objects from textual which cannot be safely imported at module level given the optional-dependency pattern. The bead description mentioned extracting BINDINGS to a dedicated section; this was interpreted as the class-level BINDINGS attribute being the dedicated section, since moving it to a separate file would require breaking the lazy-import guard.

conflict_risks: tui/__init__.py and tui/app.py are the most likely points of conflict for downstream beads importing from the tui package. The public API surface is unchanged — all symbols remain re-exported from the same package namespace.

execution_history.summary: Refactored tui/app.py from 1132 lines to 686 lines (39% reduction) by extracting three cohesive units into dedicated modules, preserving all public re-exports and observable behaviour.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
